'''Mandara-ji''' (曼荼羅寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Zentsuji Sect|ja|真言宗善通寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Zentsuji Sect|qq=}} located in {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|WD=Q191300|lt=Zentsuji|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, Kagawa Prefecture, Japan. It is the 72nd temple in the {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|WD=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku Pilgrimage|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}}. The temple is also known as Gabai-shi-zan Enmei-in (我拜師山延命院). Its principal image is {{ill|Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai|ja|金剛界大日如来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大日如来|1=Vairocana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q239847}}.
* '''Mantra''': "On Abiraunken Basaradatoban" * '''Go-eika''' (pilgrimage song): "Even a glance at the Mandala shall free one from returning twice or thrice again."
== History == According to temple tradition, Mandara-ji was founded in 596 CE as a clan temple of the {{ill|Saeki Clan|ja|佐伯氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Clan|qq=}}, the family of {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (Kōbō Daishi). Originally known as '''Seisaka-ji''' (世坂寺), the temple was later re-established by Kūkai after his return from Tang China. He enshrined the {{ill|Mandala of the Two Realms|en|Mandala of the Two Realms|WD=Q468113|lt=Mandala of the Two Realms|1=Mandala of the Two Realms}} and dedicated the main image of Dainichi Nyorai in honor of his mother, {{ill|Tamayori Gozen|ja|玉依御前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayori Gozen|qq=}}. The temple was then renamed Mandara-ji.
During the Kamakura period, the temple prospered, receiving territorial grants from {{ill|Emperor Go-Horikawa|en|Emperor Go-Horikawa|WD=Q357221|lt=Emperor Go-Horikawa|1=Emperor Go-Horikawa}}. However, the temple suffered destruction during the wars of the Sengoku period, including a fire set by Miyoshi Jitsunaga in 1560. It was later restored during the early Edo period.
The temple's famous "Kasamatsu" (umbrella pine), said to have been planted by Kūkai, was cut down in 2002 due to damage from pine wilt disease.
== Buildings == [[File:Mandaraji okunoin 01.jpg|thumb|From the precincts of Mandara-ji, the former Oku-no-in of {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}} can be seen.]] * '''Main Gate''' (仁王門) * '''Main Hall''': The principal image of Dainichi Nyorai is displayed annually during the spring equinox. * '''Daishi Hall''': Dedicated to Kūkai, where his image can be worshipped. * '''Kannon Hall''': Houses a standing statue of Sho Kannon (Prefectural Cultural Property) and a golden image of Dainichi Nyorai. * '''Goma Hall''': Features a central seated statue of Fudō Myōō and smaller wooden statues of the Five Great Myōō. * '''Hachiman Shrine''': Reconstructed in 1990, includes a main and worship hall. * '''Jizō Hall''': Contains a small painted standing statue of Jizō Bosatsu. * '''Aizen Hall''': A small vermillion shrine dedicated to Aizen Myōō. * Other structures include various Jizō statues, a stone Dainichi Nyorai, and a Fukurokuju statue.
The precincts include the "Sleeping Stone of Saigyō" and a related memorial stone inscribed with a poem attributed to him. A paid parking area is available near the main gate.
== Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property === * '''Wooden Standing Statue of Sho Kannon''': Designated in 1955 (Showa 30). Late Heian period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/kenkyoui/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/culturalassets/culturalassets_3.html |title=Cultural Assets in Kagawa |access-date=2023-06-28 |publisher=Kagawa Prefecture}}</ref>
== Access == ; By Train * 4.5 km from {{ill|Zentsūji Station|en|Zentsūji Station|WD=Q899921|lt=Zentsuji Station|1=Zentsūji Station}} (JR Shikoku Dosan Line) ; By Road * 0.5 km from {{ill|Kagawa Prefectural Route 48|ja|香川県道48号善通寺詫間線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefectural Route 48|1=Kagawa Prefectural Road Route 48|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667552}} * 6.2 km from {{ill|Zentsuji Interchange|ja|善通寺インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsuji Interchange|1=Zentsuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10923476}} (Takamatsu Expressway)
== Nearby Temples == ; {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|WD=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku Pilgrimage|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}} : 71 {{ill|Iyadan-ji|lt=Iyadan-ji|lt=Iwayaji|ja|弥谷寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwayaji|qq=}} -- (3.5 km) -* '''72 Mandara-ji''' -- (0.5 km) -- 73 {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}}

{{Japan temple infobox | Name = Mandara-ji (曼荼羅寺) | image = Mandarazi 0ø.jpg | image size = 250 | image caption = Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|WD=Q191300|lt=Zentsuji|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, Yoshihara Town, Mandara-ji 1380-1, Kagawa Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|13|23.9|N|133|45|0.79|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mandara-ji}} | Mountain name = Gabai-shi-zan (我拜師山) | Temple name = Enmei-in (延命院) | Sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}} | School = Shingon Zentsuji Sect (真言宗善通寺派) | Rank = Special Head Temple | Principal image = {{ill|Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai|ja|金剛界大日如来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大日如来|1=Vairocana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q239847}} | Founded = Traditionally attributed to 596 CE (Empress Suiko's reign) | Founder = Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyoki|1=Gyōki}} | Cultural properties = Standing statue of Sho Kannon (Kagawa Prefectural Cultural Property) | Website = [http://mandaraji.xsrv.jp Mandara-ji Official Website] }}
'''Mandara-ji''' (曼荼羅寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Zentsuji Sect|ja|真言宗善通寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Zentsuji Sect|qq=}} located in {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|WD=Q191300|lt=Zentsuji|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, Kagawa Prefecture, Japan. It is the 72nd temple in the {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|WD=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku Pilgrimage|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}}. The temple is also known as Gabai-shi-zan Enmei-in (我拜師山延命院). Its principal image is {{ill|Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai|ja|金剛界大日如来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大日如来|1=Vairocana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q239847}}.
* '''Mantra''': "On Abiraunken Basaradatoban" * '''Go-eika''' (pilgrimage song): "Even a glance at the Mandala shall free one from returning twice or thrice again."
== History == According to temple tradition, Mandara-ji was founded in 596 CE as a clan temple of the {{ill|Saeki Clan|ja|佐伯氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Clan|qq=}}, the family of {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (Kōbō Daishi). Originally known as '''Seisaka-ji''' (世坂寺), the temple was later re-established by Kūkai after his return from Tang China. He enshrined the {{ill|Mandala of the Two Realms|en|Mandala of the Two Realms|WD=Q468113|lt=Mandala of the Two Realms|1=Mandala of the Two Realms}} and dedicated the main image of Dainichi Nyorai in honor of his mother, {{ill|Tamayori Gozen|ja|玉依御前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayori Gozen|qq=}}. The temple was then renamed Mandara-ji.
During the Kamakura period, the temple prospered, receiving territorial grants from {{ill|Emperor Go-Horikawa|en|Emperor Go-Horikawa|WD=Q357221|lt=Emperor Go-Horikawa|1=Emperor Go-Horikawa}}. However, the temple suffered destruction during the wars of the Sengoku period, including a fire set by Miyoshi Jitsunaga in 1560. It was later restored during the early Edo period.
The temple's famous "Kasamatsu" (umbrella pine), said to have been planted by Kūkai, was cut down in 2002 due to damage from pine wilt disease.
== Buildings == [[File:Mandaraji okunoin 01.jpg|thumb|From the precincts of Mandara-ji, the former Oku-no-in of {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}} can be seen.]] * '''Main Gate''' (仁王門) * '''Main Hall''': The principal image of Dainichi Nyorai is displayed annually during the spring equinox. * '''Daishi Hall''': Dedicated to Kūkai, where his image can be worshipped. * '''Kannon Hall''': Houses a standing statue of Sho Kannon (Prefectural Cultural Property) and a golden image of Dainichi Nyorai. * '''Goma Hall''': Features a central seated statue of Fudō Myōō and smaller wooden statues of the Five Great Myōō. * '''Hachiman Shrine''': Reconstructed in 1990, includes a main and worship hall. * '''Jizō Hall''': Contains a small painted standing statue of Jizō Bosatsu. * '''Aizen Hall''': A small vermillion shrine dedicated to Aizen Myōō. * Other structures include various Jizō statues, a stone Dainichi Nyorai, and a Fukurokuju statue.
The precincts include the "Sleeping Stone of Saigyō" and a related memorial stone inscribed with a poem attributed to him. A paid parking area is available near the main gate.
== Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property === * '''Wooden Standing Statue of Sho Kannon''': Designated in 1955 (Showa 30). Late Heian period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/kenkyoui/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/culturalassets/culturalassets_3.html |title=Cultural Assets in Kagawa |access-date=2023-06-28 |publisher=Kagawa Prefecture}}</ref>
== Access == ; By Train * 4.5 km from {{ill|Zentsūji Station|en|Zentsūji Station|WD=Q899921|lt=Zentsuji Station|1=Zentsūji Station}} (JR Shikoku Dosan Line) ; By Road * 0.5 km from {{ill|Kagawa Prefectural Route 48|ja|香川県道48号善通寺詫間線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefectural Route 48|1=Kagawa Prefectural Road Route 48|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667552}} * 6.2 km from {{ill|Zentsuji Interchange|ja|善通寺インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsuji Interchange|1=Zentsuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10923476}} (Takamatsu Expressway)
== Nearby Temples == ; {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|WD=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku Pilgrimage|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}} : 71 {{ill|Iyadan-ji|lt=Iyadan-ji|lt=Iwayaji|ja|弥谷寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwayaji|qq=}} -- (3.5 km) -* '''72 Mandara-ji''' -- (0.5 km) -- 73 {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}}
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Saigyō Hermitage|ja|西行庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigyō Hermitage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shinko-in|ja|信香院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinko-in|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Mizukami Shrine|ja|水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizukami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yoshihara Daishi Hall|ja|吉原大師堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshihara Daishi Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sagi-i Shrine|ja|鷺井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi-i Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōike Pond|ja|大池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōike Pond|1=Q11436898|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436898}} * {{ill|Ushigaku-ji|ja|牛額寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushigaku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

{{Japan temple infobox | Name = Mandara-ji (曼荼羅寺) | image = Mandarazi 0ø.jpg | image size = 250 | image caption = Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|WD=Q191300|lt=Zentsuji|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, Yoshihara Town, Mandara-ji 1380-1, Kagawa Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|13|23.9|N|133|45|0.79|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mandara-ji}} | Mountain name = Gabai-shi-zan (我拜師山) | Temple name = Enmei-in (延命院) | Sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134368473}} | School = Shingon Zentsuji Sect (真言宗善通寺派) | Rank = Special Head Temple | Principal image = {{ill|Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai|ja|金剛界大日如来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大日如来|1=Vairocana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q239847}} | Founded = Traditionally attributed to 596 CE (Empress Suiko's reign) | Founder = Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyoki|1=Gyōki}} | Cultural properties = Standing statue of Sho Kannon (Kagawa Prefectural Cultural Property) | Website = [http://mandaraji.xsrv.jp Mandara-ji Official Website] }}
'''Mandara-ji''' (曼荼羅寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Zentsuji Sect|ja|真言宗善通寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Zentsuji Sect|qq=}} located in {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|WD=Q191300|lt=Zentsuji|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, Kagawa Prefecture, Japan. It is the 72nd temple in the {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|WD=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku Pilgrimage|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}}. The temple is also known as Gabai-shi-zan Enmei-in (我拜師山延命院). Its principal image is {{ill|Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai|ja|金剛界大日如来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大日如来|1=Vairocana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q239847}}.
* '''Mantra''': "On Abiraunken Basaradatoban" * '''Go-eika''' (pilgrimage song): "Even a glance at the Mandala shall free one from returning twice or thrice again."
== History == According to temple tradition, Mandara-ji was founded in 596 CE as a clan temple of the {{ill|Saeki Clan|ja|佐伯氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Clan|qq=}}, the family of {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (Kōbō Daishi). Originally known as '''Seisaka-ji''' (世坂寺), the temple was later re-established by Kūkai after his return from Tang China. He enshrined the {{ill|Mandala of the Two Realms|en|Mandala of the Two Realms|WD=Q468113|lt=Mandala of the Two Realms|1=Mandala of the Two Realms}} and dedicated the main image of Dainichi Nyorai in honor of his mother, {{ill|Tamayori Gozen|ja|玉依御前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayori Gozen|qq=}}. The temple was then renamed Mandara-ji.
During the Kamakura period, the temple prospered, receiving territorial grants from {{ill|Emperor Go-Horikawa|en|Emperor Go-Horikawa|WD=Q357221|lt=Emperor Go-Horikawa|1=Emperor Go-Horikawa}}. However, the temple suffered destruction during the wars of the Sengoku period, including a fire set by Miyoshi Jitsunaga in 1560. It was later restored during the early Edo period.
The temple's famous "Kasamatsu" (umbrella pine), said to have been planted by Kūkai, was cut down in 2002 due to damage from pine wilt disease.
== Buildings == [[File:Mandaraji okunoin 01.jpg|thumb|From the precincts of Mandara-ji, the former Oku-no-in of {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}} can be seen.]] * '''Main Gate''' (仁王門) * '''Main Hall''': The principal image of Dainichi Nyorai is displayed annually during the spring equinox. * '''Daishi Hall''': Dedicated to Kūkai, where his image can be worshipped. * '''Kannon Hall''': Houses a standing statue of Sho Kannon (Prefectural Cultural Property) and a golden image of Dainichi Nyorai. * '''Goma Hall''': Features a central seated statue of Fudō Myōō and smaller wooden statues of the Five Great Myōō. * '''Hachiman Shrine''': Reconstructed in 1990, includes a main and worship hall. * '''Jizō Hall''': Contains a small painted standing statue of Jizō Bosatsu. * '''Aizen Hall''': A small vermillion shrine dedicated to Aizen Myōō. * Other structures include various Jizō statues, a stone Dainichi Nyorai, and a Fukurokuju statue.
The precincts include the "Sleeping Stone of Saigyō" and a related memorial stone inscribed with a poem attributed to him. A paid parking area is available near the main gate.
== Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property === * '''Wooden Standing Statue of Sho Kannon''': Designated in 1955 (Showa 30). Late Heian period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/kenkyoui/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/culturalassets/culturalassets_3.html |title=Cultural Assets in Kagawa |access-date=2023-06-28 |publisher=Kagawa Prefecture}}</ref>
== Access == ; By Train * 4.5 km from {{ill|Zentsūji Station|en|Zentsūji Station|WD=Q899921|lt=Zentsuji Station|1=Zentsūji Station}} (JR Shikoku Dosan Line) ; By Road * 0.5 km from {{ill|Kagawa Prefectural Route 48|ja|香川県道48号善通寺詫間線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefectural Route 48|1=Kagawa Prefectural Road Route 48|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11667552}} * 6.2 km from {{ill|Zentsuji Interchange|ja|善通寺インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsuji Interchange|1=Zentsuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10923476}} (Takamatsu Expressway)
== Nearby Temples == ; {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|WD=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku Pilgrimage|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}} : 71 {{ill|Iyadan-ji|lt=Iyadan-ji|lt=Iwayaji|ja|弥谷寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwayaji|qq=}} -- (3.5 km) -* '''72 Mandara-ji''' -- (0.5 km) -- 73 {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}}
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Saigyō Hermitage|ja|西行庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saigyō Hermitage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shinko-in|ja|信香院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinko-in|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Mizukami Shrine|ja|水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizukami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Yoshihara Daishi Hall|ja|吉原大師堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshihara Daishi Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Sagi-i Shrine|ja|鷺井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagi-i Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōike Pond|ja|大池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōike Pond|1=Q11436898|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436898}} * {{ill|Ushigaku-ji|ja|牛額寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushigaku-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== References == * {{Cite book |author=宮崎建樹 |title=四国遍路ひとり歩き同行二人 |year=2007 |edition=8th |publisher=へんろみち保存協力会}}
== External Links == * [http://mandaraji.xsrv.jp/ Mandara-ji Official Website] * [https://www.88shikokuhenro.jp/72mandaraji/ Shikoku Pilgrimage Official Website - Mandara-ji] * [http://7kasyomairi.sakura.ne.jp/ Seven-Temple Pilgrimage Official Website] ==References== {{reflist}}

{{Japan temple infobox | Name = Mandara-ji (曼荼羅寺) | image = Mandarazi 0ø.jpg | image size = 250 | image caption = Main Hall | Location = {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|qid=Q191300|lt=Zentsuji|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, Yoshihara Town, Mandara-ji 1380-1, Kagawa Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{coord|34|13|23.9|N|133|45|0.79|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mandara-ji}} | Mountain name = Gabai-shi-zan (我拜師山) | Temple name = Enmei-in (延命院) | Sect = {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|古義真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|1=Kogi Shingon-shū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q134368473}} | School = Shingon Zentsuji Sect (真言宗善通寺派) | Rank = Special Head Temple | Principal image = {{ill|Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai|ja|金剛界大日如来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大日如来|1=Vairocana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q239847}} | Founded = Traditionally attributed to 596 CE (Empress Suiko's reign) | Founder = Traditionally attributed to {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|qid=Q1136664|lt=Gyoki|1=Gyōki}} | Cultural properties = Standing statue of Sho Kannon (Kagawa Prefectural Cultural Property) | Website = [http://mandaraji.xsrv.jp Mandara-ji Official Website] }}
'''Mandara-ji''' (曼荼羅寺) is a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Zentsuji Sect|ja|真言宗善通寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Zentsuji Sect|qq=}} located in {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|qid=Q191300|lt=Zentsuji|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, Kagawa Prefecture, Japan. It is the 72nd temple in the {{ill|Shikoku Pilgrimage|en|Shikoku Pilgrimage|qid=Q1972399|lt=Shikoku Pilgrimage|1=Shikoku Pilgrimage}}. The temple is also known as Gabai-shi-zan Enmei-in (我拜師山延命院). Its principal image is {{ill|Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai|ja|金剛界大日如来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongokai Dainichi Nyorai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大日如来|1=Vairocana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q239847}}.
* '''Mantra''': "On Abiraunken Basaradatoban" * '''Go-eika''' (pilgrimage song): "Even a glance at the Mandala shall free one from returning twice or thrice again."
== History == According to temple tradition, Mandara-ji was founded in 596 CE as a clan temple of the {{ill|Saeki Clan|ja|佐伯氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Clan|qq=}}, the family of {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (Kōbō Daishi). Originally known as '''Seisaka-ji''' (世坂寺), the temple was later re-established by Kūkai after his return from Tang China. He enshrined the {{ill|Mandala of the Two Realms|en|Mandala of the Two Realms|qid=Q468113|lt=Mandala of the Two Realms|1=Mandala of the Two Realms}} and dedicated the main image of Dainichi Nyorai in honor of his mother, {{ill|Tamayori Gozen|ja|玉依御前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayori Gozen|qq=}}. The temple was then renamed Mandara-ji.
During the Kamakura period, the temple prospered, receiving territorial grants from {{ill|Emperor Go-Horikawa|en|Emperor Go-Horikawa|qid=Q357221|lt=Emperor Go-Horikawa|1=Emperor Go-Horikawa}}. However, the temple suffered destruction during the wars of the Sengoku period, including a fire set by Miyoshi Jitsunaga in 1560. It was later restored during the early Edo period.
The temple's famous "Kasamatsu" (umbrella pine), said to have been planted by Kūkai, was cut down in 2002 due to damage from pine wilt disease.
== Buildings == [[File:Mandaraji okunoin 01.jpg|thumb|From the precincts of Mandara-ji, the former Oku-no-in of {{ill|Shusshaka-ji|ja|出釈迦寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusshaka-ji|qq=}} can be seen.]] * '''Main Gate''' (仁王門) * '''Main Hall''': The principal image of Dainichi Nyorai is displayed annually during the spring equinox. * '''Daishi Hall''': Dedicated to Kūkai, where his image can be worshipped. * '''Kannon Hall''': Houses a standing statue of Sho Kannon (Prefectural Cultural Property) and a golden image of Dainichi Nyorai. * '''Goma Hall''': Features a central seated statue of Fudō Myōō and smaller wooden statues of the Five Great Myōō. * '''Hachiman Shrine''': Reconstructed in 1990, includes a main and worship hall. * '''Jizō Hall''': Contains a small painted standing statue of Jizō Bosatsu. * '''Aizen Hall''': A small vermillion shrine dedicated to Aizen Myōō. * Other structures include various Jizō statues, a stone Dainichi Nyorai, and a Fukurokuju statue.
The precincts include the "Sleeping Stone of Saigyō" and a related memorial stone inscribed with a poem attributed to him. A paid parking area is available near the main gate.
== Cultural Properties == === Kagawa Prefecture Designated Tangible Cultural Property === * '''Wooden Standing Statue of Sho Kannon''': Designated in 1955 (Showa 30). Late Heian period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.kagawa.lg.jp/kenkyoui/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/culturalassets/culturalassets_3.html |title=Cultural Assets in Kagawa |access-date=2023-06-28 |publisher=Kagawa Prefecture}}</ref>
== Access == ; By Train * 4.5 km from {{ill|Zentsūji Station|en|Zentsūji Station|qid=Q899921|lt=Zentsuji Station|1=Zentsūji Station}} (JR Shikoku Dosan Line) ; By Road * 0.5 km from {{ill|Kagawa Prefectural Route 48|ja|香川県道48号善通寺詫間線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefectural Route 48|1=Kagawa Prefectural Road Route 48|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667552}} * 6.2 km from {{ill|Zentsuji Interchange|ja|善通寺インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsuji Interchange|1=Zentsuji interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10923476}} (Takamatsu Expressway)
